items.title: Match the checkpoint activation
items.avoid: Changing `hidden_act` or swapping GELU for ReLU when fine-tuning pretrained Transformers.
items.prefer: Read the model config (`hidden_act: gelu`) and use the framework's GELU module in FFN layers.
items.why: Weight matrices were optimized under the original activation's output distribution and gradient profile.
items.example: In Hugging Face: `model.config.hidden_act` → ensure `BertIntermediate` uses the same activation class before training.

items.title: Pick one GELU implementation end-to-end
items.avoid: Training with exact erf GELU but exporting to a runtime that only implements the tanh approximation without checking parity.
items.prefer: Run a parity check on a random tensor; align `approximate` flags across PyTorch, ONNX, and serving runtimes.
items.why: Small activation differences compound through depth and can shift softmax margins in classification heads.
items.example: Compare `F.gelu(x, approximate='tanh')` vs `approximate='none'` on 1e4 random values; max abs diff should be documented before deployment.

items.title: Use GELU where Transformers expect it
items.avoid: Defaulting every new MLP to GELU without considering compute budget or ReLU baselines.
items.prefer: Use GELU in Transformer FFN blocks and NLP/ViT stacks; benchmark ReLU for lightweight CNNs or latency-critical paths.
items.why: GELU's benefit is most documented in deep attention models; elsewhere the cost may outweigh gains.
items.example: A 12-layer BERT encoder with 3072-dim FFN applies GELU millions of times per forward pass—profile before blindly replacing ReLU in a small tabular MLP.
